A Few Filter Tricks: Drop All the Falsy Values in One Line

We reach for JavaScript's array `filter` method all the time to pull out the elements we care about — say, every number bigger than 10. It's one of the most-used array methods out there. In this post I'll show a few small tricks for filtering out falsy values, plus how to do the same thing when you're working with arrays of objects.
How to Stop Users From Selecting Specific Elements
Sometimes you want a specific section on your site to be unselectable by the user. Here are a few ways to do that.
Generate a UUID Right in the Browser — No npm Package Needed

When you're writing a blog post or a quick demo, you almost always end up faking some data — and that fake data usually needs a unique value, the classic key/id. This post walks through how to generate a unique key/id without pulling in any package at all, using the browser's built-in UUID support.
A Casual Chat About TypeScript — What It Is and Why Use It

TypeScript has basically become a must-have skill for a lot of developers, but plenty of projects are still on JavaScript and on the fence about migrating. So this post is a casual share of my own take on TypeScript and why you'd want to use it.
A Friendly Look at Functional Programming in JavaScript: Currying

Functional Programming has this one really neat idea called Currying. In this post I'll walk through what Currying actually is, how to build it from scratch, and where you might use it in real code. Spoiler: it's not some fancy new syntax — it's just closures doing their thing.
Prettier Tutorial: You'll End Up Using It Anyway — Why Not Start Now? (Full VSCode Setup)

Prettier is one of those frontend tools you have to know. Even if you're not using it today, you'll end up using it eventually — so why not start now? This post walks through installing Prettier, configuring it, setting it up in VSCode, and how it differs from ESLint.
A Few Tricks for Wrapping localStorage So It Stops Annoying You

When you're building web apps, you reach for localStorage to store data all the time — but the localStorage API itself is honestly kind of clunky. In this post I'll show a few tricks for wrapping it so it's nicer to use, covering both the Function and Class approaches, plus how to dodge the usual JSON.parse errors.
Why a Higher-Order Function Doesn't Work in Vue SFC @click Handlers

A friend recently asked me about a fun quirk in Vue — they were trying to use a Higher-Order Function (HOF) in a Vue Single-File Component and ran into a pretty interesting gotcha. So here's a post breaking down what's going on.
Fixing the 'quill Overwriting modules/xxx with class...' Warning

A quick note on the "quill Overwriting modules/xxx with class..." warning I ran into while using Quill, and how I fixed it. If you're toggling a Quill editor with v-if or switching routes in an SPA and your console keeps spamming this message, this is for you.
The Fake jQuery Trick That'll Have Your Coworkers Scratching Their Heads

A fun little trick to confuse your coworkers — make them think they're using real jQuery when it's actually just plain JavaScript. We'll fake out the `$()` selector, the `.on()` event helper, and even `hide()` / `show()`, all with vanilla JS. A reminder, too, that jQuery itself is just JavaScript under the hood.
